generated_comment: You know, sometimes I wonder if these new policies are actually practical. Like this push for menstrual leave alongside the extended maternity benefits... *yaar*, it just means the rest of us have to pick up the slack. Always the men covering for everything. Is that really fair to the department, *aur* the officers who are constantly holding the fort?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment encodes the surface manifestation of questioning the fairness of menstrual/maternity leave policies and framing them as a burden on men and the organization. It uses 'fair/unfair' framing and implicitly links women's reproductive choices/biology to reduced productivity and increased workload for male colleagues.
persona_text: You are Ashok. Age 54. Married. A senior IAS officer posted in Bhopal. Your department has just been told that a woman officer five years junior to you has been selected for a prestigious central deputation that you had also applied for. You are sitting in your government quarter in the evening having a whisky with your batchmate who is also an IAS officer. You are telling him about the selection and what you think really drove it.
